subject: Social and Political Life– II
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze the complete supply chain of a shirt with real data
Understand profit distribution and inequalities in markets
Explore ethical trade, worker challenges, and sustainability
Develop research, critical thinking, and presentation skills
Relate market systems to global and local economic realities

lesson_plan.title: Detailed Shirt Production Analysis
topics_covered: Stages: cotton farming, ginning, spinning, weaving, tailoring, retail
Costs, time, and labor involved at each stage
teaching_strategy: Flowcharts and diagrams with real data
Class discussion on challenges and impacts
student_activities: Create a detailed flowchart of production
Include costs, labor, and time at each stage

lesson_plan.title: Profit & Loss Calculations
topics_covered: Estimating costs and profits at each stage
Analyzing inequalities among workers, traders, and retailers
teaching_strategy: Class discussion on cost and profit distribution
Use of sample numerical data for calculations
student_activities: Identify stages with the largest and smallest profits

lesson_plan.title: Field Study: Local Market Investigation
topics_covered: Local shops, markets, and sourcing of shirts
Comparison of textbook and real-life data
teaching_strategy: Guided fieldwork with observation and interviews
student_activities: Interview sellers about costs, prices, and sourcing
Prepare a report comparing observations with chapter content

lesson_plan.title: Design an Ethical Brand
topics_covered: Fair wages, safe working conditions, and sustainability
Creating a business model considering ethical practices
teaching_strategy: Class discussion on ethical trade practices
Guided creative work for brand design
student_activities: Create a brand ensuring fair wages and safety
Design logo, supply chain, and marketing plan

lesson_plan.title: Debate: Who Gains Most?
topics_covered: Economic inequalities in the shirt market
Arguments supported by research and calculations
teaching_strategy: Use of real or assumed data to support arguments
student_activities: Divide into groups: farmers, workers, traders, retailers
Conduct a class debate and discuss fairness
assessment: Quality and use of data in arguments

lesson_plan.title: Global and Sustainability Analysis
topics_covered: Comparison of local and global markets
Environmental and social sustainability in clothing production
teaching_strategy: Research-based discussion with charts and reports
Guided reflection on global market and sustainability issues
student_activities: Compare local vs global shirt production and trade
Identify environmental issues and propose sustainable practices
Present findings with charts or diagrams

teaching_aids: Charts and diagrams of shirt supply chain
Access to internet or reference books for research
Role cards, props, and visual aids for debates
teacher_tip: Encourage independent research, real-world observation, and critical thinking. Use debates, field visits, and creative brand projects to engage students with ethics, economics, and sustainability.
